questions.subject: Geography
questions.prompt: How many of the following pairs of tropical cyclone terminology and their corresponding regions are correctly matched?
contextLines: 2. Typhoon : Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched (Hurricanes occur in the North Atlantic, Eastern North Pacific, and South Pacific). Pair 2 is incorrectly matched (Typhoons occur in the Western North Pacific). Pair 3 is incorrectly matched (Willy-willies occur in the Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean). Pair 4 is correctly matched (Cyclones occur in the Indian Ocean). Thus, only two pairs (1 and 4) are correct.
questions.takeaway: The regional terminology for tropical cyclones includes Hurricanes (Atlantic/East Pacific/South Pacific), Typhoons (West Pacific), Cyclones (Indian Ocean), and Willy-willies (Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Terms for Tropical Cyclones: Hurricane – North Atlantic, Eastern North Pacific and South Pacific Ocean. Cyclone – Indian Ocean. Typhoon – Western North Pacific Ocean. Willy-willy: Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.prompt: With reference to the conditions required for the formation of tropical cyclones, consider the following statements: 1. They can initiate over both warm ocean waters and heated coastal landmasses. 2. A sea surface temperature greater than 26 degrees Celsius is a prerequisite for their formation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as tropical cyclones always form over oceans, not landmasses. Statement 2 is correct as they require a sea surface temperature greater than 26 degrees Celsius.
questions.takeaway: Tropical cyclones strictly require warm ocean waters (SST > 26°C) for their genesis and do not form over land.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Always form over oceans where sea surface temperature is greater than 26°C.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the regional terminology of tropical cyclones: 1. A tropical storm originating in the Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean is locally referred to as a Willy-willy. 2. The term Typhoon is exclusively used for tropical cyclones forming in the North Atlantic Ocean. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; tropical cyclones are called Willy-willies in the Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean. Statement 2 is incorrect; Typhoons occur in the Western North Pacific Ocean, while those in the North Atlantic are called Hurricanes.
questions.takeaway: Cyclone terminology varies by ocean basin: Hurricanes (North Atlantic/Eastern Pacific), Typhoons (Western North Pacific), and Willy-willies (Eastern Southern Indian Ocean).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Terms for Tropical Cyclones: Hurricane – North Atlantic... Typhoon – Western North Pacific Ocean. Willy-willy: Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean.
